title: High-Grade Vaginal Sarcoma (ER/PR+) Diagnosed During and After Pregnancy
description: A 31-year-old gravida 4 para 0 African American woman at 22-weeks gestation expelled an 11 cm pelvic mass with pathology showing a highly cellular spindle-cell tumor; the differential included endometrial stromal sarcoma, undifferentiated uterine sarcoma, and primary vaginal sarcoma. She underwent cesarean hysterectomy at 36 weeks with benign final pathology, but a high-grade ER/PR-positive vaginal sarcoma recurred at 3 months postpartum and was treated with upper vaginectomy and HDR vaginal brachytherapy, with 58-month disease-free survival.
notes: Derived from dataset row idx 177170. The original dataset row is copied into case_07_source.json. All patient data comes from the synthetic augmented-clinical-notes dataset. The primary diagnostic challenge is distinguishing primary vaginal sarcoma from endometrial stromal sarcoma and undifferentiated uterine sarcoma in a pregnant patient, with a pregnancy-interval benign hysterectomy pathology preceding confirmed postpartum recurrence.
